title: GPU Architecture & Kernel Optimization
learningObjectives: Explain the CUDA thread hierarchy (grid → block → warp → thread), derive flattened thread IDs for 1D and 2D grids, and map the 3DGS tile rasterizer launch configuration to this model.
Define the SIMT execution contract, predict the cost of warp divergence for a given conditional, and identify divergence-free kernel patterns.

Derive the CGMA ratio for the naive matrix-multiply kernel, explain why it limits achieved throughput to <6% of peak on the G80, and show how shared-memory tiling multiplies CGMA by the tile dimension.
State the memory coalescing rule for 32-thread warps, classify a memory access pattern as coalesced or uncoalesced, and estimate the transaction penalty for stride-N access.
Construct a roofline chart for a target GPU, place a kernel on it given arithmetic intensity and achieved FLOP/s, and prescribe whether compute or memory optimization should be pursued.

Instrument a PyTorch/CUDA training loop with torch.profiler and NVTX, export a Chrome/Perfetto trace, and identify the dominant kernel, CPU–GPU sync gaps, and memory transfer bottlenecks from the timeline.

readings.title: The CUDA Programming Model: Grids, Blocks, Warps & the SIMT Contract
The GPU Memory Hierarchy: CGMA Ratio, Coalescing & Shared-Memory Tiling
Kernel Profiling & Tracing: torch.profiler, NVTX & Perfetto

lab.title: Profiling the 3DGS Rasterizer with torch.profiler & Perfetto
